Meta.title: Fuse Beads Assistant
Meta.description: A multilingual fuse bead assistant with theme switching and pattern conversion.

Home.title: Prepare every bead pattern with a clearer workflow
Home.description: Import an image and turn it into a preview, coded chart, and color summary in one workspace, with theme and language controls ready to use.
Home.cardBody: Import an image, map it to the Mard 221 palette, and export a preview, coded chart, and build-ready pattern.
Home.pwaBody: Install the app to mobile or desktop home screens and launch it with a more native-like experience across iOS, Android, and Windows.
Home.routeBody: Control the board size and image area separately, with square-first output, centered placement, and padded edges when needed.
Home.langBody: Merge nearby shades with a tolerance slider to cut down the number of bead colors you need to prepare.

PatternStudio.paletteLoadError: Failed to load the palette file. Please make sure public/Mard221.csv is available.
PatternStudio.generationError: Pattern generation failed. Please re-import the image and try again.
PatternStudio.imageReadError: Failed to read the image. Please try a PNG, JPG, or WEBP file.
PatternStudio.title: Turn any image into a buildable fuse bead pattern
PatternStudio.description: Import an image, set the board and image area, choose a sampling mode, and generate a preview, coded chart, and color summary.
PatternStudio.accentPeachDesc: Warm and soft for a handcrafted feel
PatternStudio.accentTealDesc: Cool and calm for a studio mood
PatternStudio.accentAmberDesc: Bright and energetic for tutorial flows
PatternStudio.settingsDesc: Import an image, then adjust the board, image area, and color conversion options.
PatternStudio.uploadDesc: PNG, JPG, and WEBP are supported. Clear subjects and distinct color blocks work best.
PatternStudio.uploadHint: The generated plan uses the bead width and height you set here, not the source pixel size.
PatternStudio.imageTitleHint: The title appears above the source image and carries into the large export page.
PatternStudio.widthHint: Horizontal bead count for the final pattern. 24-96 is a good starting range.
PatternStudio.heightHint: Vertical bead count for the final pattern. Higher values preserve more detail.
PatternStudio.heightSquareHint: Square-first mode is on, so height stays synced with width.
PatternStudio.boardPresetsHint: Switch common board sizes quickly. The default starts at 52 x 52.
PatternStudio.preferSquare: Prefer square output (pad rectangles with H2)
PatternStudio.imageAreaWidthHint: The subject is centered inside this area first, then centered again on the board.
PatternStudio.imageAreaHeightHint: Turn off the image ratio lock to tune the image area height separately.
PatternStudio.imageAreaHeightLockedHint: The image ratio is locked, so height follows the source aspect ratio automatically.
PatternStudio.backgroundTagHint: You can enter a custom color tag. The default is H1; if the tag does not exist, it falls back to H1 automatically.
PatternStudio.fitHint: Contain adds padding, cover crops edges, and stretch fills the whole grid.
PatternStudio.samplingModeHint: Precise sampling aims for the smallest color difference from the source image, while smooth sampling follows browser-style scaling more closely.
PatternStudio.colorMergeToleranceHint: Higher values merge nearby colors into the same bead color, which reduces the total number of colors.
PatternStudio.zoomHint: Each cell is currently shown at {size}px for easier inspection.
PatternStudio.zoomGestureHint: After clicking the chart, use the mouse wheel or a two-finger gesture to zoom. The scrollbars below still stay available for panning.
PatternStudio.zoomGestureDescription: Wheel to zoom, drag to pan, click outside to exit
PatternStudio.workspaceDesc: Switch between the preview, coded chart, and source image, with zoom and drag support in the chart views.
PatternStudio.previewEmpty: After you import an image, the Mard 221 color preview will appear here.
PatternStudio.dropImageDescription: Drag a PNG, JPG, or WEBP image here to replace the current source and regenerate the preview right away.
PatternStudio.planEmpty: Once a chart is generated, the coded grid view will appear here.
PatternStudio.planColorsEmpty: Generate a chart first to see the coded chart with color counts.
PatternStudio.planColorsLegend: Color counts under the coded chart
PatternStudio.sourceEmpty: No image has been imported yet. Choose one from the left panel.
PatternStudio.colorStatsDesc: See which colors are used in this chart and how many beads each one needs.
PatternStudio.colorStatsEmpty: The most-used colors will appear here after a chart is generated.
PatternStudio.guideDesc: Use this area to quickly check the board size, image area, and active conversion mode.
PatternStudio.usageBody: This structure is ready for future features like color reduction, board splitting, and print pagination.

PatternExport.title: Full pattern export
PatternExport.description: This page renders an unconstrained large image that includes the coded chart and the color bead summary below it.
PatternExport.viewerModeDescription: Wheel to zoom, drag to pan, click outside to exit
PatternExport.missingData: No exportable pattern data was found. Please go back to the pattern generator and create a chart first.
PatternExport.generationError: Failed to generate the large export. Please return to the pattern generator and try again.
PatternExport.paletteLoadError: Failed to load the palette file. Please make sure public/Mard221.csv is available.

PwaStatus.readyDescription: This workspace can reopen without a connection after the first successful load.
PwaStatus.offlineDescription: Cached pages, palette data, and your saved image data are still available here.

OfflinePage.title: You are offline, but the cached workspace is still available
OfflinePage.description: If this device opened the app successfully before, you can continue working from the cached pages. Reconnect once if you need a page or asset that has never been loaded before.
OfflinePage.paletteBody: The Mard 221 palette file is cached, so pattern generation can keep working after the first successful load.
OfflinePage.imageBody: Images already stored in this browser tab flow or IndexedDB remain available while the browser keeps the site data.
OfflinePage.tipsBody: If you have never opened a certain route or the browser cleared its storage, reconnect once to refresh the offline cache.
